title: equisetum arvense var. agreste
intro: Equisetum arvense var. agreste, commonly known as field horsetail, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is characterized by its hollow, jointed stems and spore-bearing cones. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its high content of silica and other minerals. The health benefits of field horsetail include treating urinary tract issues, kidney stones, and arthritis, as well as promoting healthy bones and hair growth. Field horsetail can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
